binaries.title: ksrext.sys (Windows Secure Kernel Runtime Extension / Soft Restart) - No security-relevant change: loader-callback locking refactor and pool-API modernization

changed_functions.name: KsrRegisterLoaderCallbacks
key_changes: Refactored the loader-callback interface from hold-pushlock-across-callback to a reference-count drain model: register/unregister spins on the exclusive KsrpLoaderInterfaceLock and, while KsrpLoaderInterfaceRefCount != 0, releases the lock and blocks in ExBlockOnAddressPushLock (0x14000B134) on KsrpLoaderInterfaceEvent/KsrpLoaderInterfaceRefCount before modifying the table

On the unregister (arg1=NULL) path, installs concrete default handlers (KsrDbPersistPagesWithMetadata, KsrDbPersistMemoryPartition, ... at 0x14000B2D4..0x14000B34C) instead of clearing the pointers; the unpatched path clears seven routine pointers with individual stores (0x1C0007ECD..0x1C0007F04)
Expanded callback table: accepted structure size check raised from 0x58 to 0x88; additional function pointers written at [rdi+58]..[rdi+78] and [rdi+80]
Added a KsrSecureKernelConnected / dword_1400072A4 & 0x10 guard before KsrDbpDetachDatabase/KsrDbpAttachDatabase
changed_functions.security_notes: No security impact. In the unpatched build every consumer (KsrPersistMemoryWithMetadata, KsrQueryMetadata, KsrClaimPersistedMemory, KsrEnumeratePersistedMemory, KsrFreePersistedMemory, KsrFreePersistedMemoryBlock, KsrPersistMemoryPartition) reads its routine pointer under KsrpLoaderInterfaceLock SHARED, NULL-checks it, and invokes it via __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr WHILE STILL HOLDING the shared lock, releasing only after the call returns (e.g. call at 0x1C0007218 precedes ExReleasePushLockSharedEx at 0x1C0007229). The unregister path clears the pointers under the EXCLUSIVE form of the same lock (acquired 0x1C0007DB3). Because shared and exclusive acquisitions are mutually exclusive, the teardown cannot clear a pointer between a consumer's read and its call, so no TOCTOU race window exists; and a cleared pointer is NULL-checked, so it is not a NULL dereference. The patched build achieves the same safety property with a different strategy (drop the lock during the callback, keep a reference count, drain it before teardown). This is a concurrency refactor, not a fix for a demonstrable unpatched defect.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED unregister path clears 7 pointers under EXCLUSIVE KsrpLoaderInterfaceLock:
00000001C0007ECD  mov   cs:KsrpLoaderRegistered, bl              ; bl = 0
00000001C0007EDA  mov   cs:KsrpPersistMemoryWithMetadataRoutinePointer, rbx  ; rbx = 0
00000001C0007EE1  mov   cs:KsrpPersistMemoryPartitionRoutinePointer, rbx
00000001C0007EE8  mov   cs:KsrpClaimPersistentMemoryRoutinePointer, rbx
00000001C0007EEF  mov   cs:KsrpFreePersistentMemoryRoutinePointer, rbx
00000001C0007EF6  mov   cs:KsrpFreePersistentMemoryBlockRoutinePointer, rbx
00000001C0007EFD  mov   cs:KsrpEnumeratePersistentMemoryRoutinePointer, rbx
00000001C0007F04  mov   cs:KsrpQueryMetadataRoutinePointer, rbx

; UNPATCHED consumer reads+invokes under SHARED lock, releases after the call:
00000001C00071D5  call  cs:__imp_ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx
00000001C00071E1  mov   rax, cs:KsrpPersistMemoryWithMetadataRoutinePointer
00000001C00071F0  test  rax, rax                                 ; NULL-checked
00000001C0007218  call  cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr           ; lock still held
00000001C0007229  call  cs:__imp_ExReleasePushLockSharedEx       ; released after call

; PATCHED register/unregister drains the reference count before modifying the table:
000000014000B126  lea   rdx, KsrpLoaderInterfaceRefCount
000000014000B12D  lea   rcx, KsrpLoaderInterfaceEvent
000000014000B134  call  cs:__imp_ExBlockOnAddressPushLock
; then installs concrete default handlers instead of clearing:
000000014000B2D4  lea   rax, KsrDbPersistPagesWithMetadata
000000014000B2E1  mov   cs:KsrpPersistMemoryWithMetadataRoutinePointer, rax

changed_functions.name: KsrMmAllocatePagesForMdlEx
key_changes: Migrated the MDL pool allocation from ExAllocatePoolWithTag(0x200, ..., 'Boot') at 0x1C0008B8C to ExAllocatePool2(0x42, ..., 'Boot') at 0x14000C010; the size expression lea edx, ds:30h[r15*8] ((page_count << 3) + 0x30) is byte-identical in both builds
Added btr r13d, 0x1F (0x14000BF46) with test ebp,ebp / jns (0x14000BFA2) to route a bit-31 flag straight to the tracing fallback allocator
Added 2 MB large-page support: cmp rbx, 0x200000 (0x14000BF7E) selecting large-page mode, served by the new helper KsrpAllocateLargePagesFromMemoryRange (0x14000C0A8)
changed_functions.security_notes: No security impact. The MDL size arithmetic is identical across builds (r15 = TotalBytes >> 0xC, then lea edx, ds:30h[r15*8], unpatched 0x1C0008B84 / patched 0x14000C008), so the switch to ExAllocatePool2 does not change any overflow or truncation behaviour of the size computation. ExAllocatePool2 appears at 46 call sites across the patched build and 0 in the unpatched build, identifying a systematic API modernization rather than a targeted overflow fix. The physical page-fill loop only writes as many PFN entries as pages are actually available from the reserve, so the size expression is not converted into an out-of-bounds write. The bit-31 flag routing and large-page path are new functionality.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED size + allocator:
00000001C0008B7A  shr   r15, 0Ch
00000001C0008B75  mov   ecx, 200h
00000001C0008B84  lea   edx, ds:30h[r15*8]        ; (page_count << 3) + 0x30
00000001C0008B8C  call  cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag

; PATCHED same size expression, different allocator API:
000000014000BFF9  shr   r15, 0Ch
000000014000BFFD  mov   ecx, 42h
000000014000C008  lea   edx, ds:30h[r15*8]        ; identical arithmetic
000000014000C010  call  cs:__imp_ExAllocatePool2

changed_functions.name: KsrpMmReplenishPages (unpatched @ 0x1C0008574)
key_changes: Adjusted the reserve-replenishment starting physical addresses
Migrated allocations from ExAllocatePoolWithTag to ExAllocatePool2
Independent min/max physical-address tracking
changed_functions.security_notes: Affects which physical pages populate the reserve; no attacker-reachable memory-safety impact. The report's earlier label of this function as 'Page Database Population' was incorrect; the function is KsrpMmReplenishPages.

changed_functions.name: KsrMmFreePagesFromMdl
key_changes: Added a large-page (MDL flag 0xB) path using MmFreeMemoryRanges instead of per-page bitmap clearing
Locking moved from inline KeEnterCriticalRegion + ExAcquirePushLockExclusiveEx to helper functions

changed_functions.name: KsrConnectSecureLoader
key_changes: Unified the conditional MDL unlock path (single-page vs multi-page) into one cleanup helper
Migrated the callback-structure allocation to ExAllocatePool2
Adjusted callback-table field offsets
changed_functions.security_notes: The single-page MDL pointer computation is correct in both builds; the change is code cleanup.

changed_functions.name: KsrQueryMetadata
key_changes: Rewritten to use the reference-count / helper functions instead of inline pushlock operations
Added a flag-bit check before the metadata helper

changed_functions.name: KsrPersistMemoryPartition
key_changes: Locking moved to helper functions
Memory-range calculation preserved
ExAllocatePoolWithTag replaced with ExAllocatePool2

changed_functions.name: KsrCleanupPageDatabase
key_changes: Pushlock operations moved to helper functions
Added a flag check before ExNotifyCallback

changed_functions.name: KsrClaimPersistedMemory
key_changes: Reference-count helpers replace inline pushlock use
Added parameter validation rejecting arg5 values with any bit other than bit 0 set (test ebp, 0xFFFFFFFE at 0x14000A18E)
Added a flag-bit check
changed_functions.security_notes: The added arg5 check is a defensive parameter/contract validation, not a memory-safety fix.

changed_functions.name: sub_1c0001010 (bitmap operation)
key_changes: Register allocation and code generation differences only

summary: No demonstrable, reachable security vulnerability is fixed by this patch. The two originally suspected patterns are not supported by the binaries. (1) The 'callback deregistration race' in KsrRegisterLoaderCallbacks does not exist: in the unpatched build every consumer reads its routine pointer under KsrpLoaderInterfaceLock SHARED and invokes it via __guard_dispatch_icall_fptr while STILL HOLDING the shared lock (call at 0x1C0007218 precedes the release at 0x1C0007229; the same ordering holds for all seven consumers), while the unregister path clears the pointers under the EXCLUSIVE form of the same lock. Shared and exclusive are mutually exclusive, so there is no read/clear/call window, and consumers NULL-check the pointer so a cleared value is not a NULL dereference. The patch swaps this lock-across-callback model for a reference-count drain (KsrpAcquire/ReleaseLoaderInterfaceReference plus ExBlockOnAddressPushLock on KsrpLoaderInterfaceRefCount) and installs concrete default handlers on unregister; this is a concurrency refactor. (2) The 'MDL integer overflow' in KsrMmAllocatePagesForMdlEx is not fixed by the ExAllocatePool2 migration because the size expression lea edx, ds:30h[r15*8] is byte-identical in both builds; ExAllocatePool2 is used at 46 sites across the patched build (0 in the unpatched build), i.e. a driver-wide API modernization, accompanied by new bit-31 flag routing and 2 MB large-page support. Both findings are downgraded to no security-relevant change.
